hypothesis: If crystal material property prediction is performed using a cross-modal predictive architecture that jointly leverages graph, text, and X-ray diffraction spectral representations through cross-modal latent space prediction and contrastive learning, then prediction accuracy (measured by MAE) will surpass single-modality baselines such as CGCNN, CoGN, and ALIGNN, because integrating complementary structural, compositional, and diffraction information from multiple modalities captures richer material representations than any single modality alone.
